publications.title: Case study of GW190425 for classifying binary neutron star versus binary black hole mergers and constraining asymmetric dark matter with gravitational wave detectors
publications.authors: Khadkikar, Sanika; Singh, Divya
publications.authors_highlighted: Khadkikar, Sanika; Singh, Divya
publications.abstract: We explore the possibility that GW190425 could have originated from black holes that merged after dark matter accretion caused their progenitor neutron stars to implode. Using this event, we place constraints on dark matter parameters, such as its mass and interaction cross section. We simulate GW190425-like events and analyze them using future gravitational wave detector networks. Next-generation observatories like the Einstein Telescope and Cosmic Explorer recover the tidal signature even for soft, compact stars, enabling confident classification.

publications.title: Precise and accurate neutron star radius measurements with next-generation gravitational wave detectors
publications.authors: Khadkikar, Sanika; Gupta, Ish; Kashyap, Rahul; Chandra, Koustav; Gamba, Rossella; Sathyaprakash, Bangalore S.
publications.authors_highlighted: Khadkikar, Sanika; Gupta, Ish; Kashyap, Rahul; Chandra, Koustav; Gamba, Rossella; Sathyaprakash, Bangalore S.
publications.abstract: We use Bayesian inference to quantify the precision with which neutron star radius can be inferred with upgrades in current gravitational wave detectors and next-generation observatories such as the Einstein Telescope and Cosmic Explorer. Next-generation observatories can resolve the radius precisely and accurately, across most of the mass range to within 5% for both soft and stiff equations of state.

publications.title: Quasi-stationary sequences of hyper-massive neutron stars with exotic equations of state
publications.authors: Khadkikar, Sanika; Mangat, Chatrik Singh; Banik, Sarmistha
publications.authors_highlighted: Khadkikar, Sanika; Mangat, Chatrik Singh; Banik, Sarmistha
publications.abstract: We study the effect of differential rotation, finite temperature and strangeness on quasi-stationary sequences of hyper-massive neutron stars. We report that the addition of hyperons to the EoS produces a significant boost to the spin-up phenomenon.

publications.title: Maximum mass of compact stars from gravitational wave events with finite-temperature equations of state
publications.authors: Khadkikar, Sanika; Raduta, Adriana R.; Oertel, Micaela; Sedrakian, Armen
publications.authors_highlighted: Khadkikar, Sanika; Raduta, Adriana R.; Oertel, Micaela; Sedrakian, Armen
publications.abstract: We deduce an upper limit on the maximum mass of static, cold neutron stars from GW170817 using finite-temperature equations of state, and show that accounting for finite temperature relaxes previously derived constraints on the maximum mass of a cold, static compact star.
